title: SSH Weak Ciphers and Key Exchange Algorithms
description: OpenSSH 4.7p1 on the target supports weak cryptographic algorithms including RC4 (arcfour) ciphers and diffie-hellman-group1-sha1 key exchange. These are considered cryptographically weak and susceptible to various attacks. The SSH version itself (4.7p1) is severely outdated with multiple known CVEs.
evidence:
SSH configuration analysis via MCP:
- Weak cipher: arcfour/RC4 enabled (severity: high)
- Weak KEX: diffie-hellman-group1-sha1 enabled (severity: medium)
- SSH version: OpenSSH 4.7p1 Debian 8ubuntu1 (protocol 2.0)
- Host keys: 1024-bit DSA, 2048-bit RSA
remediation: Upgrade OpenSSH to a current supported version. Disable weak ciphers (arcfour, DES, RC4) and weak KEX algorithms (diffie-hellman-group1-sha1) in sshd_config. Use only AES-GCM or ChaCha20-Poly1305 ciphers. Use curve25519-sha256 or diffie-hellman-group16-sha512 for key exchange.

suggestions: Add command output demonstrating access or data extraction
Include session output or file access proof
